notes: Camera movement is finicky; can be allieviated by turning mouse sensitivity all the way down. Some models do not load, others load partway, and some maps will hardlock the game.

title: VRChat

notes:
Tutorial worked fine. After that the game would get stuck on black screen when loading certain worlds. This can be avoided by joining a different world on launch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CWgHJ519B3U Audio worked in desktop mode. Performance wasn't any different from Windows. Avatars loaded fine, some didn't show up at all, though.

VR mode was also tested. Performance here was pretty much the same as on Windows. However, audio didn't work here.
title: VRChat

notes: VR doesn't work at all, in Desktop mode some materials are invisible and on loading some worlds the game stops working and just displays a black screen. Also the mouse glitches slightly to the bottom right all the time (unity wide)

title: VRChat

notes: Worked without issue, but now I cannot get past clicking on the "GO" button. Doing so makes the game freeze. I have tried setting my launch options to different places (e.g vrchat://launch?id=wrld_78373831-0109-4808-9b63-27382f4c6975) but no luck.
title: VRChat

notes: The game runs, but the audio is kind of crackly, opening the menu requires you to alt-tab to regain mouse control (and then again after you close the menu to fix the mouse sensitivity), the mouse likes to drift to the bottom right. This is even after applying the fixes suggested here. https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/1199
title: VRChat

notes:
With D3D11 & DXVK enabled: loads up to login and loading screen but freezes to black.
With D3D11 disabled: window loads magenta screen.
With Wined3D enabled: window loads black screen.
title: VRChat

notes:
(Tested non VR only) 

It runs the fist time fine, after setting up you can enter the hub. But it seems like that the "home" world won't load, all you get is a black screen. To bypass this you can load into the hub. Set the following launch option within steam: vrchat://launch?id=wrld_b51f016d-1073-4c75-930d-9f44222c7fc3:7085

Another problem is, that sometimes downloading a world gets stuck. I noticed this problem only when using proton. When running VRchat outside of steam, using wine-staging and DXVK I don't have this problem.

You can run VRchat outside of steam but you need to bypass the home world again. You can do this with the same launch option in the terminal. Open the terminal inside the VRchat folder and use this command; wine VRChat.exe vrchat://launch?id=wrld_b51f016d-1073-4c75-930d-9f44222c7fc3:7085

This way VRchat runs the best for me and seems more stable than using Proton. Just make sure you have installed DXVK.  

This launch option with the world ID my change over time. If it doesn't work, replace the world ID with an other world from the VRchat website. 

If you need other launch options, you can check this website. https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1240103533
